abstract: An atom describes the default imaging characteristics for all the panoramic nodes in a scene.
overview:
## Overview

A panorama-imaging atom describes the default imaging characteristics for all the panoramic nodes in a scene. This atom overrides QuickTime VR’s own defaults.

The panorama-imaging atom has an atom type of `kQTVRPanoImagingAtomType` (`'impn'`). Generally, there is one panorama-imaging atom for each imaging mode, so the atom ID, while it must be unique for each atom, is ignored. QuickTime VR iterates through all the panorama-imaging atoms.

The structure of a panorama-imaging atom is defined by the `QTVRPanoImagingAtom` data type:

The imagingValidFlags field in the panorama-imaging atom structure specifies which imaging property fields in that structure are valid. You can use these bit flags to specify a value for that field:

**Constant descriptions**

items.description: The major version number of the file format.
items.name: majorVersion

items.description: The minor version number of the file format.
items.name: minorVersion

items.description: The imaging mode to which the default values apply.
items.name: imagingMode

items.description: A set of flags that indicate which imaging property fields in this structure are valid.
items.name: imagingValidFlags

items.description: The default correction mode for panoramic nodes.
items.name: correction

items.description: The default imaging quality for panoramic nodes.
items.name: quality

items.description: The default direct-drawing property for panoramic nodes.
items.name: directDraw

items.description: An atom that contains a string for QuickTime VR.
items.name: QTVR string atom

items.description: An atom that contains name for the entire scene, the default node ID, and default imaging properties, as well as a list of the nodes contained in the QTVR track.
items.name: VR world atom container

items.description: An atom contains the name of the scene and the default node ID to be used when the file is first opened.
items.name: VR world header atom

items.description: An atom that is the parent atom of one or more node-specific imaging atoms.
items.name: Imaging parent atom

title: Panorama imaging atom
